business_ideas.idea: Real estate syndication on Twitter—raise capital from audience for deals
Tax optimization consulting for $30-500M net worth investors
Carwash marketplace/advisory brokerage (following Real Estate Twitter model)
Crypto infrastructure tools—Twilio/Stripe equivalents for web3 builders
Guided crypto learning tours (Disneyland-style, not course, handheld mentor model)

predictions.text: Crypto infrastructure companies will be as critical as Twilio/Stripe in internet era
Anonymous Twitter accounts managing billions will become dominant wealth builders

disagreements: Sam remains skeptical NFT valuations could collapse like CryptoKitties; Shaan bullish after immersion. Sam prioritizes boring-business opportunities; both agree infrastructure > collectibles.
dynamics: Sam drives real-estate-Twitter trend-spotting with data (4-6 figure Twitter followers managing $2B+ AUM); Shaan extends into crypto learnings and philosophy. Shaan more immersion-focused (allocates $250K budget, involves team), Sam more analytical/skeptical. Strong alignment on 'boring is opportunity' theme.
frameworks: Think Week à la Bill Gates — weekly immersion learning sprints
Work-like-a-lion sprint weeks for intensive learning, not grazing
Disney World mapping — sectors/niches to explore depth-wise
Learning by immersion with trusted guides vs. courses
Picks and shovels not gold — infrastructure over collectibles
Social agreement gives value to all assets (Bitcoin, NFTs, art, money)
Rarity and scarcity as value drivers, not intrinsic utility
Boring assets = underserved Twitter niches = opportunity
learnings: Industries with low Twitter/content presence have exponential opportunity because fewer are documenting practices
New wealth builders (anonymous, outside traditional networks) are winning more than established influencers in crypto
Infrastructure businesses in emerging ecosystems create more durable value than collectible plays
NFT and Bitcoin value derives from collective belief and social agreement, like all art and money
Holders refusing to sell despite 80% crashes signals genuine adoption vs. speculation
Learning by doing with expert guides more effective than courses or passive content
Immersion reveals opportunities invisible at surface level; one week shifts understanding more than passive learning for months
Tax and financial strategy is massive underserved market for high-net-worth creators

banger_quotes.quote: Crypto punks are Picassos—original, finite supply, social agreement gives value
Bitcoin adoption faster than mobile phones or internet adoption
Fewer people blogging your industry = more opportunity to own narrative
This week I'm just sitting in garage 18 hours a day, neck deep in crypto

numbers_dropped: Strip Mall Guy: $500M AUM, $3M personal cash flow/year, 4-person team
Moses Kagan: $200M AUM, $40M from Twitter followers (one $20M investor)
Keith Wasserman: ~$2B AUM, 1000 investors, Twitter 5X'd fund size
Nick Huber: Storage units, friend and portfolio company
Shaan's crypto week learning budget: $250K personal allocation
Rookie NFT: 45 min after mint, Shaan's purchase went 5X in two days
CryptoKitties 2017: 80% of Ethereum network traffic; dead within year
notable: Episode centers on 'boring is profitable' trend across real estate Twitter ($2B+ AUM managed by 4-6 anonymous accounts). Remarkable Andre origin story (bed/wheelchair-bound 2 years, recovered, now on team). Shaan allocated $250K personal budget to crypto immersion week, treating learning as investment. Bill Gates think-week framework applied to content. Long immersive format (2h+) unusual for MFM, suggesting deep learning conversation.
